summary:
Hyperbaric oxygen therapy may have a role as an adjunctive treatment in select cases of necrotizing fasciitis, potentially improving outcomes through better tissue perfusion and antimicrobial effects, but lacks robust clinical证据 supporting its use. It should be used cautiously, only when patient-stable and within specific contexts such as refractory disease or after surgery, rather than routinely.
